title: Slow-cooker Moroccan lamb tagine
subtitle: Spiced lamb with chickpeas and dried apricots
excerpt: Diced lamb shoulder slow-cooked with ras el hanout, cinnamon, tinned tomatoes, chickpeas, and dried apricots for six to eight hours. The spices soften and deepen over the long cook without losing their warmth. Serve with couscous.
sourceNotes: Tagine is both the North African slow-braised dish and the conical clay pot it traditionally cooks in. The slow cooker is a close functional equivalent: long, low, covered, with minimal evaporation. The sweet and savoury combination of lamb, dried fruit, and warm spices is a Moroccan signature that arrived in British home cooking via restaurants and Yotam Ottolenghi's influence in the 2000s. Ras el hanout (a North African spice blend of up to 30 ingredients) is widely available in UK supermarkets.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freezes well for up to 3 months. The chickpeas soften further on reheating.
recipe.batchNotes: Double the recipe — tagine is an ideal batch cook and the flavour improves over several days.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Better the next day. The spices settle into the sauce as it rests.

content.text: Ras el hanout varies between brands. Taste a pinch before adding the full quantity — some blends are very strong and others are mild. Start with 1 tablespoon if you are using a new brand and adjust next time. The dried apricots dissolve partially into the sauce and provide sweetness that balances the spice without making the dish taste of fruit.

Add the {{lamb-shoulder}}, {{chickpeas-tinned}}, {{tinned-tomatoes}}, {{dried-apricots}}, {{onion}}, {{garlic}}, {{ginger-root}}, {{ras-el-hanout}}, {{cinnamon-ground}}, {{turmeric}}, {{tomato-puree}}, {{honey}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}} to the slow cooker. Pour in the {{stock-chicken}} and stir to combine. Add the {{preserved-lemon}} rind if using. Cook on low for 6 to 8 hours, until the lamb is completely tender. Taste and adjust the seasoning — the sauce should taste warm, slightly sweet, and deeply spiced. Serve over couscous with a dollop of Greek yoghurt.

items.symptom: Tagine tastes bland despite the spices
items.cause: Spices not bloomed before going in, or ras el hanout is old
items.fix: Fry the ras el hanout, cinnamon, and turmeric in a small splash of oil in a pan for 1 minute before adding to the slow cooker. Spices lose potency quickly — replace if they are more than a year old

items.cause: Apricots are very sweet, or too much honey
items.fix: Add a squeeze of lemon juice and taste. The preserved lemon rind also balances sweetness if you have it available

items.cause: Diced smaller than 4 cm or cooker running cool
items.fix: Continue for another hour. Lamb shoulder needs the full cook time to break down the collagen in the connective tissue

content.text: Moroccan lamb tagine has become a British dinner party and midweek standard. The slow cooker version requires no monitoring and produces a deeply flavoured result that would otherwise need a full afternoon on the hob. It keeps well in the fridge for three days and reheats without deteriorating.
